name: ACTUATE THERAPEUTICS, INC.
business: Actuate Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing therapies for difficult-to-treat cancers through inhibition of GSK-3. Its lead product candidate, elraglusib, is being evaluated for first-line metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma and other cancers. The company has both injectable and oral formulations in development.
sellsTo: Pediatric cancer patients with Ewing sarcoma and neuroblastoma
Patients with advanced refractory solid tumors

exposures.sentence: We are developing elraglusib, an ATP-competitive small molecule that is designed to enter cancer cells and block the function of the enzyme glycogen synthase kinase-3 beta (GSK-3), a master regulator of complex biological signaling cascades, including those mediated by oncogenes, that lead to tumor cell survival, growth, migration, and invasion.

catalysts.event: FDA and EMA meeting to discuss Phase 3 global registration study design
catalysts.sentence: During the first half of 2026, we plan to meet with the FDA and EMA to discuss the design and execution of a Phase 3 global registration study to support potential product registration.

catalysts.event: Final data from IIT evaluating elraglusib with FOLFIRINOX and losartan
catalysts.sentence: We expect to receive all final data from this study, which is considered exploratory and may inform future development of elraglusib in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Annual incidence of pancreatic cancer in the US expected to exceed 67,000 patients in 2026
tamClaims.sentence: According to the American Cancer Society, the annual incidence of pancreatic cancer is expected to exceed 67,000 patients in the United States in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Global pancreatic cancer treatment market was $2.9 billion in 2024, expected to grow to $5.8 billion by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: The Pancreatic Cancer Treatment Market Size Report, 2030 by Grand View Research, estimates the current global market for treating pancreatic cancer was approximately $2.9 billion in 2024 and is expected to grow to $5.8 billion by 2030 based on the growing aging population and associated rise in lifestyle-related diseases.
